titles: Original Istrian Charm with Modern Comfort
descriptions: A charming stone villa in Damijanići near Žminj — the perfect base for exploring Istria.
Enjoy the private pool, garden, jacuzzi, and peaceful outdoor space for long summer days.
A comfortable stay for families and smaller groups with space to relax, dine, and unwind together.
Traditional architecture, a warm interior, and authentic details create a true Istrian stay.

About.title: Overview of Villa Bergi
About.des: The luxurious Villa Bergi is located in the small, charming village of Damijanići, just 2 km from Žminj, an Istrian town with a rich history. Villa Bergi is a 300-year-old stone house that has been owned by the same family for centuries. The villa has been renovated in accordance with the traditional Istrian style and the former way of life of local farming families.

the_space.title: The Space
the_space.des: The villa is suitable for 12 people and has 5 bedrooms and 4 bathrooms. The living room and kitchen are decorated in a combination of Istrian and modern style. The villa has a fireplace, a green garden, a swimming pool, and a barbecue area where you can spend quality relaxing time with friends and family. On the wall of the house there is a portrait, above which is the inscription San Giacomo (St. James). It is still unknown who placed the stone head on the wall of the house, but it is believed that it was installed by the owner during the construction of the house.
the_space.des2: GENERAL: Air conditioning, fireplace, heating, washing machine, sofa bed, swimming pool, wireless internet, linen included, towels included. KITCHEN AND DINING ROOM: Stove, coffee machine, dishes and utensils, oven. ENTERTAINMENT AND OTHER AMENITIES: TV, satellite/cable, outdoor grill, lawn/garden, radio, jacuzzi.

guest_access.title: Guest Access
guest_access.des: ATTRACTIONS AND OTHER ACTIVITIES: Marina, wine roads, fishing, diving, cycling, sailing, casino, beach, aquapark, ski lift Poreč, horse riding, golf, visits to historical sights, bike rental, olive oil tours. PARKING IS FREE AND ON SITE.

locations.title: What to visit:
des: - Explore the beautiful islands of Brijuni National Park, rich in natural beauty and cultural heritage.
- Discover the ancient Roman amphitheater in Pula, one of the best-preserved in the world.
- Dive into the underwater world at Pula Aquarium, near the city center. Explore diverse marine habitats, observe marine species, and learn about ocean conservation.
- Experience thrilling slides, lazy rivers, and relaxing pools at Istralandia water park near Novigrad. Perfect for families and water lovers — a fun day for all ages.

Villa_Description.des: The luxurious Villa Bergi is located in the small, charming village of Damijanići. Damijanići is located 2 km (1.2 miles) from Žminj, an Istrian town with a rich history. Villa Bergi is a 300-year-old stone house that has been owned by the same family for centuries. The villa has been renovated in accordance with the way of life of farmers and the Istrian traditional style of construction.
Villa_Description.subtitle: The Space
Villa_Description.des2: The villa is suitable for 12 people, has 5 bedrooms and 4 bathrooms, the living room and kitchen are decorated in a combination of Istrian and modern style. The villa has a fireplace, a green garden, a swimming pool, a barbecue where you can spend quality relaxing time with friends and family. On the wall of the house there is a portrait, above which is the inscription San Giacomo (St. James). It is still unknown who placed the stone head on the wall of the house, but it is believed that it was installed by the owner during the construction of the house. GENERAL: Air conditioning, fireplace, heating, washing machine, sofa bed, swimming pool, wireless internet, linen included, towels included. KITCHEN AND DINING ROOM: Stove, coffee machine, dishes and utensils, oven. ENTERTAINMENT AND OTHER AMENITIES: TV, SATELLITE /CABLE, outdoor grill, lawn /garden, radio, jacuzzi.
Villa_Description.des3: ATTRACTIONS AND OTHER ACTIVITIES: Marina, Wine roads, Fishing, Diving, Cycling, Sailing, Casino, Beach, Aquapark, Sky lift Poreč, Horse riding, Golf, Visits to historical sights, Bike rental, Olive oil tours. PARKING IS FREE AND ON SITE.

Pricelist.title: Price List
Pricelist.subtitle: *Price refers to one night stay in euros

Payment_conditions.subtitle: PAYMENT AND BOOKING CONDITIONS
Payment_conditions.title: Payment and Booking Terms
Payment_conditions.des: Tourist tax is not included in the price!
Payment_conditions.des2: To confirm the reservation, 30% of the total amount must be paid in advance directly to the accommodation owner, and 70% upon arrival.
Payment_conditions.des3: Refundable deposit if everything is fine: 500 euros.
Payment_conditions.des4: Free cancellation within one week after booking (EXCEPT LAST-MINUTE BOOKINGS, THEN 48 HOURS). Last-minute booking – two weeks before arrival.
Payment_conditions.des5: On the last day of stay, guests must leave the accommodation by 10 a.m., so that others can check in from 4 p.m.
Payment_conditions.des6: Note: Pets are not allowed on the property.

steps.title: Send Inquiry
steps.des: Please send us an inquiry via the contact form, email, or phone with all required information and desired stay period in the Villa Relax holiday home. Expect a quick email reply and our best offer.
steps.des2: To confirm your reservation, 30% of the total price must be paid in advance, and the rest upon arrival. After payment, please send us proof, and we’ll send you a confirmation of payment and booking. There are 2 ways to pay: bank transfer or PayPal (debit and credit cards).
steps.subtitle: Bank Transfer
steps.des3: Once payment is completed, we’ll confirm your reservation. Relax and look forward to your summer stay at Villa Bergi, Istria – Croatia!

Environs.title: Žminj & Istria
Environs.des: Žminj is a picturesque Istrian town located in the center of the peninsula, known for its traditional architecture and peaceful, authentic atmosphere. Surrounded by hills, vineyards, and olive groves, it is an ideal place for relaxation and discovering Istrian culture.
Environs.des2: The villa can be reached by car or taxi. Free parking is available on-site.
Environs.subtitle: Nearby Attractions

rovinj.title1: 1. The Old Town Was an Island
rovinj.des1: Until the 18th century, Rovinj was a real island. In 1763 the Austrians filled in the channel and connected it to the mainland. That's why the streets are so narrow and winding – designed for defense, not comfort.
rovinj.des2: The Church of St. Euphemia has the tallest bell tower in Istria – 62 meters – and the statue of St. Euphemia on top rotates depending on the wind direction.
rovinj.des3: In the Old Town, many houses have doors and windows directly on the sea, typical of old Venetian fishing towns and one of Rovinj’s most iconic sights.
rovinj.des4: The batana is a small flat-bottomed wooden boat, a symbol of Rovinj’s fishing tradition. It has its own Ecomuseum listed on the UNESCO Intangible Heritage list.
rovinj.des5: Balbi’s Arch from the 17th century marks the entrance to the Old Town. It features carved heads of a Turk and a Venetian, a reminder of Venetian rule.
rovinj.des6: Golden Cape (Punta Corrente) is one of the most beautiful places for walking and cycling in Istria, known for its pine forest, coves, and crystal-clear sea.
rovinj.des7: Rovinj is considered one of the most romantic towns in Europe thanks to its sea views, picturesque narrow streets, and spectacular sunsets.

bale.title1: 1. A Stone Town with a Rich History
bale.des1: Bale is a small medieval stone town with a history dating back to Roman times. Its narrow streets and stone houses preserve the authentic spirit of Istria.
bale.des2: The town was once known as Castrum Vallis, meaning fortress in the valley. Bale used to be an important defensive and strategic center.
bale.des3: Numerous dinosaur fossils have been found in the Bale area, including remains of brachiosaurus, making it a unique place in Europe.
bale.des4: Bale is known as a town of artists, with many galleries, studios, and cultural events held throughout the year.
bale.title5: 5. Church of the Blessed Virgin Mary
bale.des5: The Parish Church of the Blessed Virgin Mary dominates the old town and is an important historical and cultural landmark.
bale.des6: The surroundings of Bale are filled with olive groves, vineyards, and natural landscapes ideal for walking, cycling, and exploring Istria.
bale.des7: Bale is a perfect destination for those seeking peace, authentic Istrian atmosphere, and an escape from mass tourism.
